pattern: Find the longest digit-prefix shared between any integer in arr1 and any integer in arr2 by hashing all number-prefixes from one array and probing with the other.
hints: Convert each integer to a string and enumerate all of its prefix strings (i.e., first 1 digit, first 2 digits, …).
Store every prefix of arr1 numbers in a hash set, then for each number in arr2 probe all its prefixes against the set, tracking the maximum matching length.
A prefix of an integer is formed by its leading digits; '100' has prefixes '1', '10', '100' — iterate length from longest to shortest or track the running max.

edge_inputs.description: single large number sharing a long prefix
all numbers in arr2 are prefixes of one number in arr1
234 is NOT a prefix of 12345 — must start from leftmost digit
overlapping digits but no numeric prefix relationship

stress.description: random integers up to 1e8 in both arrays
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    arr1 = [rng.randint(1, 10**8) for _ in range(size)]
    arr2 = [rng.randint(1, 10**8) for _ in range(size)]
    return [arr1, arr2]
